pi_items.source: iDempiere accounting documentation and source code references confirm that 'E' represents Expense account type. See: https://wiki.idempiere.org/en/Account_Element_(Chart_of_Accounts)_(Window_ID-118)

iDempiere documentation and training materials confirm 'N' represents Natural account sign, which causes the account to follow the default behavior for its type (Expense accounts naturally have Debit sign). See: https://wiki.idempiere.org/en/Training_on_Basic_Accounting_Using_Adempiere

iDempiere database uses boolean fields with 'Y' for True/Yes and 'N' for False/No. 'N' indicates this is NOT a summary account (it is a leaf account). Confirmed in multiple iDempiere source code and schema references.

This is a task-specific account code created for the benchmark task. Not found in standard GardenWorld dataset. Grep search of entire codebase confirms '76500' only appears in create_account_element task files.
This is a task-specific account name created for the benchmark task. Grep search confirms this exact string only appears in create_account_element task files (task.json and verifier.py).
This is a task-specific requirement for the account description text. Not a claim about an existing data value, but rather a test criterion.

privileged_info_summary: The task creates a new chart of accounts entry with account type 'E' (Expense), account sign 'N' (Natural), and summary flag 'N' (not a summary account). These account classification codes are standard in iDempiere: 'E' for Expense type, 'N' for Natural sign (which uses the account type's default debit/credit behavior), and 'N' for non-summary leaf accounts. The specific account code (76500) and name ('Social Media Advertising') are task-specific synthetic values created for this benchmark task and do not correspond to standard GardenWorld chart of accounts entries.
